configurations.name: Generate Lesson (OpenAI)
args: What Makes an AI System Production-Ready
From Demo AI to Real AI Systems
Why most AI demos fail in production
Define the difference between a demo, prototype, pilot, and production AI system
Explain why demo success is usually based on narrow, curated, low-noise scenarios
Identify the gap between controlled prompts and real user behavior
Describe how messy inputs, ambiguous intent, and adversarial usage expose weaknesses
Explain why production systems must survive scale, variability, and failure
Identify hidden dependencies that demos ignore, such as retrieval quality, permissions, latency, and monitoring
Describe how user trust is lost when an AI system behaves inconsistently
Explain why a single impressive interaction says little about long-term reliability
Recognize the importance of repeatability, observability, and fallback behavior
Analyze the post-demo engineering work needed to make an AI feature production-ready
Identify organizational reasons teams mistakenly ship demos as products
Evaluate an AI system and determine whether it is truly production-ready or merely demo-ready
